title: Assembly method
abstract: Apparatus for a light unit comprising a light source and an optical assembly, the optical assembly comprising at least a first and second layer of light transmissive material, wherein the first layer is proximate to the light source and the second layer is further away from the light source than the first layer; wherein the first layer has a smaller scattering coefficient with respect to the second layer; and wherein the light source is affixed to the optical assembly using a pre-determined fit.
claims:
Claims 1. Apparatus for a light unit comprising a light source and an optical assembly, the optical assembly comprising at least a first and second layer of light transmissive material, wherein the first layer is proximate to the light source and the second layer is further away from the light source than the first layer; wherein the first layer has a smaller scattering coefficient with respect to the second layer; and wherein the light source is affixed to the optical assembly. 2. Apparatus according to claim 1 , wherein the layers are arranged to coaxially align with the light source. 3. Apparatus according to claim 2, wherein the first layer is pliable and the second layer is rigid. 4. Apparatus according to claim 1 , 2 or 3, wherein the light transmissive material is arranged to positionally align the light source in a pre-determined spatial relationship with the transmissive material using a predetermined fit. 5. Apparatus according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the light source comprises a source mounted onto a substrate; and wherein the source is an LED, and the substrate is flexible such that the substrate can be mechanically deformed into a non-linear shape during the assembly of the light unit. 6. Apparatus according to claim 5, wherein the substrate is metallic or fibreglass. 7. Apparatus according to claim 6, wherein the substrate comprises a plurality of LEDâs. 8. Apparatus according to claim 6 or 7, wherein the LEDâs are OLEDâs. 9. Apparatus according to any of claims 1 to 4, wherein the light source is an electro luminescent wire. 10. Apparatus according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the optical assembly comprises a plurality of materials of differing optical and mechanical properties. 1 1 . Apparatus according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the light source is embedded within the optical assembly using an optically suitable material to provide ancillary positioning and/or thermal support to the light source. 12. A method of manufacturing a light unit comprising a light source and an optical assembly, the optical assembly comprising at least a first and second layer of a light transmissive material, the method comprising: applying a coating to the first layer such that the coating adheres to the first layer to form the second layer of the optical assembly such that the first layer and second layer are bonded together to form the optical assembly; wherein the first layer has a smaller scattering coefficient with respect to the second layer; and affixing the light source within the optical assembly such that the inner surface of the first layer is in contact with the light source. 13. A method of manufacturing a light unit according to claim 12, wherein the first layer is formed by applying a coating to a substrate such that the coating adheres to the outside surface of the substrate to form the first layer of the optical assembly. 14. A method of manufacturing a light unit according to claim 13, wherein the substrate is removed from the first layer prior to affixing the light source. 15. A method of manufacturing a light unit comprising a light source and an optical assembly, the optical assembly comprising at least a first and second layer of a light transmissive material, the method comprising: extruding a first light transmissive material to form the first layer of the optical assembly; extruding a second light transmissive material to form the second layer of the optical assembly; affixing the first layer within the second layer such that the outer surface of the first layer is in contact with the inner surface of the second layer to form the optical assembly; wherein the first layer has a smaller scattering coefficient with respect to the second layer; and 

 affixing the light source within the optical assembly such that the inner surface of the first layer is in contact with the light source. 16. A method of manufacturing a light unit according to any of claims 12 to 15, wherein the light unit has an initial shape and is subjected to a deforming process to allow plastic deformation of the optical assembly such that the assembly can be mechanically deformed into a second shape. 17. A method of manufacturing a light unit according to claim 16, wherein the light source is affixed within the optical assembly before the assembly has undergone plastic deformation and mechanically deformed into a second shape. 18. A method of manufacturing a light unit according to claim 16, wherein light source is affixed within the optical assembly after the assembly has undergone plastic deformation and mechanically deformed into a second shape. 19. A method of manufacturing a light unit according to claim 18, wherein the light source is affixed within the optical assembly using an assembly fixture. 20. A method of manufacturing a light unit according to claim 18, wherein the light source is affixed within the optical assembly by splitting the optical components of the optical assembly into multiple pieces and reassembling the pieces to affix the light source within the optical assembly. 21 . A method of manufacturing a light unit according to any of claims 12 to 20, wherein the light transmissive material is modified using a chemical or mechanical process to alter, at least one of, the mechanical or optical properties of the transmissive material. 22. A method of manufacturing a light unit according to claim 21 , wherein the mechanical or optical properties are altered by modifying the surface and/or chemical properties of the transmissive material. 23. A method of manufacturing a light unit according to claim 22, wherein the diffusion of the light transmissive material is improved by adjusting the thickness of the transmissive material depending on the light distribution characteristics of the light source. 24. Apparatus according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the light unit is mounted within structural housing and electrically coupled to an electrical driver to create a general purpose, or decorative, lighting solution.
